prompt: Write Python code defining validate_person(obj) that returns True only when obj has string name and integer age >= 0. Return only code.
harness:
assert validate_person({'name':'Ada','age':36}) is True
assert validate_person({'name':'Ada','age':-1}) is False
assert validate_person({'name':3,'age':36}) is False
assert validate_person({'age':36}) is False

prompt: Return ONLY a JSON object with keys ok (boolean) and reason (string). Set ok to true. No prose, no extra keys.
Return ONLY a JSON object describing a fictional person with exactly these keys: name (string), age (integer), tags (array of strings). No prose.
Return ONLY a JSON array of exactly 3 objects, each with keys city (string) and country (string). No prose.
Return ONLY a JSON object for a job config with keys mode (must be "fast" or "safe") and retries (integer). Choose safe defaults. No prose.
